channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: India court extends custody of opposition leader for 4 more days
item.description: An Indian court has extended custody of Arvind Kejriwal, a top opposition leader, for four more days as the nation prepares for a highly anticipated election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK comedian forced to remove hot dog from subway poster due to ban on advertising junk food
item.description: A British comedian has been ordered to remove a picture of a hot dog from a poster in London subway stations for his new standup show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuelan migrants increasingly stranded in Mexico, contributing to decline in illegal crossings to US
item.description: Venezuelan migrants often identify Mexico as the most challenging part of their journey to the U.S. border due to the country's crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinian Authority forms new Cabinet following worldwide calls for government reform
item.description: Following international calls for reform, the Palestinian Authority has announced the formation of a new Cabinet. Hamas has allegedly rejected the new government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia arrests another suspect in concert hall attack that killed 143
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's top investigative body said Thursday that another suspect has been detained as an accomplice in the attack by gunmen on a suburban Moscow concert hall that killed 143 people.
item.dc:creator: AP, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's PM requests meeting with Kim Jong Un to fix 'various bilateral problems' with North Korea
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida wants to meet with North Korea's Kim Jong Un to have the nation recognize the return of Japanese people abducted during the 1970s and 80s.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinian Authority announces a new Cabinet as it faces calls for reform
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The Palestinian Authority has announced the formation of a new Cabinet as it faces international pressure to reform.
item.dc:creator: AP, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House: Putin linking Ukraine to Moscow attack ‘nonsense,’ ‘propaganda’
item.description: The White House on Thursday offered a fiery refutation to Russian President Vladimir Putin’s claims linking the Moscow attack, carried out by ISIS-K, to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Putin says F-16s in Ukraine 'won’t change the situation on the battlefield'
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin this week said that Ukraine obtaining F-16 fighter jets “won’t change the situation on the battlefield.” Putin, speaking to Russian air force pilots Wednesday, said that Russia had no plans to attack a NATO country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sforza, The Hill

item.title: French lawmakers condemn 'bloody and murderous' 1961 massacre of Algerian protesters
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French lawmakers on Thursday condemned an infamous 1961 police crackdown on Algerian protesters in Paris as a “bloody and murderous repression,” marking another step in the country's recognition of the massacre that authorities sought to cover up for decades.
item.dc:creator: AP, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine president warns of countermeasures in response to Chinese aggression at sea
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The Philippine president said Thursday that his government would take action against what he called dangerous attacks by the Chinese coast guard and suspected militia ships in the disputed South China Sea, saying “Filipinos do not yield.” Ferdinand Marcos Jr.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's lower house passes a bill banning hair discrimination. It now goes to the Senate
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Lawmakers in France's lower house of parliament on Thursday approved a bill that would ban discrimination over the texture, length, color or style of someone's hair.
item.dc:creator: JADE LE DELEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: King Charles III in Easter message calls for kindness after cancer diagnosis
item.description: King Charles III shared a message Thursday calling for kindness and friendship “in a time of need,” after he and Kate, the Princess of Wales, were diagnosed with cancer.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: King Charles stresses importance of kindness as he skips pre-Easter service amid cancer treatment
item.description: LONDON (AP) — King Charles III on Thursday stressed the importance of friendship and acts of caring in a recorded message delivered to a traditional pre-Easter church service, which the monarch skipped as he continues to undergo cancer treatment.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers in Thailand overwhelmingly approve a bill to legalize same-sex marriage
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Lawmakers in Thailand's lower house of Parliament overwhelmingly approved a marriage equality bill on Wednesday that would make the country the first in Southeast Asia to legalize equal rights for marriage partners of any gender.
item.dc:creator: AP, NewsNation

item.title: State Department human rights staffer resigns over Biden’s Gaza policy
item.description: A State Department human rights staffer said she is resigning in response to the Biden administration's policy on Gaza in a CNN opinion piece Wednesday. “For the past year, I worked for the office devoted to promoting human rights in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: The death toll in the Moscow concert hall attack rises to 143, while 80 others remain hospitalized
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — The death toll from last week's Moscow concert hall attack rose to 143, Russian authorities said Wednesday. Around 80 other people wounded in the siege by gunmen remain hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: AP, WGN 9

item.title: UN chief calls for slavery reparations
item.description: United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres has called for slavery reparations over the transatlantic slave trade, which deprived enslaved people of “education, healthcare, opportunity and prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: UK court says Assange can't be extradited on espionage charges until US rules out death penalty
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A British court ruled Tuesday that Julian Assange can’t be extradited to the United States on espionage charges unless U.S.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan approves plan to sell fighter jets to other nations in latest break from pacifist principles
item.description: The contentious decision to allow international arms sales is expected to help secure Japan’s role in a joint fighter jet project.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Is attack in Russia a threat to the world?
item.description: Russia suffered the worst terror attack on its soil in decades Friday when assailants descended on a concert hall near Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Kate and William 'extremely moved' by support since the Princess of Wales' cancer revelation
item.description: Kate, the Princess of Wales, and her husband, Prince William, are said to be “extremely moved” by the public’s warmth and support following her shocking cancer announcement as tributes continued to pour in Sunday from around the world.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 men charged in Moscow attack, showing signs of beatings at hearing as court says 2 accept guilt
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Four men accused of staging the Russia concert hall attack that killed more than 130 people appeared before a Moscow court Sunday on terrorism charges showing signs of severe beatings. One appeared to be barely conscious during the hearing.
item.dc:creator: AP, WGN 9

item.title: Putin says gunmen who raided Moscow concert hall tried to escape to Ukraine. Kyiv denies involvement
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — The suburban Moscow music hall where gunmen opened fire on concertgoers was a blackened, smoldering ruin Saturday as the death toll in the attack surpassed 130 and Russian authorities arrested four suspects. President Vladimir Putin claimed they were captured while fleeing to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: AP, WGN 9

item.title: Heavy rains kill at least 7 in Rio de Janeiro state, 4-year-old rescued after 16 hours under mud
item.description: PETROPOLIS, Brazil (AP) — Heavy rains in Rio de Janeiro state have killed at least seven people, authorities said Saturday, while a 4-year-old girl was rescued after more than 16 hours under mud. The girl was pulled out alive in the city of Petropolis, 69 kilometers (43 miles) north of Rio.
item.dc:creator: AP, WGN 9

item.title: The Moscow concert hall attack wasn't the first during Putin's 25-year rule
item.description: The attack on a Moscow concert hall where armed men opened fire and set the building ablaze, killing at least 133 people, was the latest in a long series of bombings and sieges that have unsettled and outraged Russians during Vladimir Putin's nearly quarter-century as either prime minister or presid
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Meghan Markle and Prince Harry release statement following Princess Kate's cancer diagnosis
item.description: Meghan Markle and Prince Harry have expressed their condolences following Princess Kate's announcement of her cancer diagnosis.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kremlin says 40 killed and more than 100 wounded in attack on Moscow concert hall
item.description: Russia’s Federal Security Service says 40 people were killed and more than 100 were wounded in an attack at a Moscow concert hall. The attack, which Russian authorities are investigating as terrorism, is the deadliest in Russia in recent years. The venue is still on fire and the death toll may rise.
item.dc:creator: AP, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Princess Kate reveals she has cancer, is undergoing chemotherapy
item.description: Catherine, Princess of Wales, revealed Friday she has been diagnosed with cancer and is undergoing chemotherapy.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC
